name: session-review
description: Review-episode protocol that resumes valid context and reserves clean audit mode for independence-required edges.

interface.shortDescription: Coordinate worker/reviewer review loops with audit or fast self-mode.
interface.longDescription: Runs an internal session-review protocol where a worker and reviewer converge code or document work using wiki snapshot audit mode or fast self-mode, parseable status blocks, lock enforcement, and profile-aware completion gates. Existing review episodes should be resumed when their target remains valid; clean audit context is reserved for independence-required reviews.
defaultPrompt: Use $doctor to diagnose backend, vault, and git readiness without creating persistent configuration.
Use $request-review to start or resume a review loop; do not create a clean episode merely to repeat the same context.
Use $review to inspect the requested review branch.
Choose audit mode when independent context is required and fast self-mode for lower-risk feedback loops.
Use $complete after reviewer approval and the selected completion gate.
